title: Define profile classes
outcome: Four profile classes (local-macos-full, headless-dev, agent-safe, workstreams-maintainer) defined in .chezmoidata/profiles.toml, each declaring render/exclude categories + gui/secrets/remote_writes booleans. Exclusions enforced by a templated .chezmoiignore driven off the profile's excludes list; dot_zshrc/dot_gitconfig now consume the declarative profile data. agent-safe excludes prompt/session/runtime/editor-gui/agent-tools/secrets and is secrets=false, gui=false, remote_writes=false.
validation: validate.sh PASS — all 4 profiles defined; agent-safe excludes secrets+editor-gui with hard-block booleans false; .chezmoiignore profile-driven; template balance OK; no secrets; chezmoi-render DEFERRED (binary not installed)
decisions: epic's `data/profiles/` maps to chezmoi-native auto-loaded `.chezmoidata/profiles.toml` (single source defining all four)
exclusion is enforced via a templated `.chezmoiignore` (chezmoi's real render/exclude mechanism), not just declared in prose
secrets category target set to ~/.config/wfos-secrets/** (distinct path) so per-profile exclusion does not clobber all of ~/.config
templates gate on declarative profile booleans (remote_writes, secrets) rather than hardcoding profile-name checks
token_note: loaded 1 story block + STATE + ledger tail; no canon ref needed (S2 manifest names none); compact PASS/FAIL gate output
